zh.lifeSummary: Hugh du Plessis Baron of Arundel是John du Plessis 7th Earl of Warwick和Christian Sanford的兒子，於1237年出生在英國素可阿盧德爾 卡素。他於1249年10月與Isabel Biset在英國史洛普爾斯托達結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1292年8月7日在英國史洛普爾斯托斯登享年55歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭奧可福爾胡可 諾頓聖 比特也斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Hugh du Plessis Baron of Arundel是John du Plessis 7th Earl of Warwick和Christian Sanford的儿子，于1237年出生在英国素可阿盧德爾 卡素。他于1249年10月与Isabel Biset在英国史洛普爾斯托達结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1292年8月7日在英国史洛普爾斯托斯登享年55岁，埋葬在英国英格兰奧可福爾胡可 諾頓聖 比特也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: du
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: Hugh du Plessis Baron of Arundel was born in 1237, in Arundel Castle, Sussex, England as the son of John du Plessis 7th Earl of Warwick and Christian Sanford. He married Isabel Biset in October 1249, in Stottesdon, Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died on 7 August 1292, in Stottesden, Shropshire, England, at the age of 55, and was buried in St Peter's Church, Hook Norton, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Hugh du Plessis Baron of Arundel est né en 1237, à Arundel Castle, Sussex, Angleterre, fils de John du Plessis 7th Earl of Warwick et Christian Sanford. Il a épousé Isabel Biset en octobre 1249, à Stottesdon, Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 7 août 1292, à Stottesden, Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterré à St Peter's Church, Hook Norton, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Er hat im Oktober 1249 in Stottesdon, Shropshire, England Isabel Biset geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 7. it.lifeSummary: Hugh du Plessis Baron of Arundel è nato il nel 1237 nel Arundel Castle, Sussex, England come figlio di John du Plessis 7th Earl of Warwick e Christian Sanford. Lui sposato Isabel Biset nel ottobre 1249, nel Stottesdon, Shropshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì il 7 agosto 1292, nel Stottesden, Shropshire, England, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolto a St Peter's Church, Hook Norton, Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugh du Plessis Baron of Arundelは1237年にイギリススセックスアルンデル カストルでJohn du Plessis 7th Earl of WarwickとChristian Sanfordの息子として生まれた。1249年10月にイギリスシュロプシュアイアストッテスドンでIsabel Bisetと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1292年8月7日にイギリスシュロプシュアイアストッテスデンで55歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアホーオク ノートンスト ペテル'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.Saint Peters Church, Hook Norton, Cherwe: 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 훅 노르턴 스트 피터'스 처르치
ko.St Peters Church, Hook Norton, Oxfordshi: 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 훅 노르턴 스트 피터'스 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: Hugh du Plessis Baron of Arundel(은)는 1237년 영국 수세스 아런들 카스틀에서 John du Plessis 7th Earl of Warwick(와)과 Christian Sanford의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1249년 10월 영국 슈럽실르 스토츠던에서 Isabel Biset(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1292년 8월 7일 영국 슈럽실르 스토츠든에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 훅 노르턴 스트 피터'스 처르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Hugh du Plessis Baron of Arundel nasceu em 1237, em Arundel Castle, Sussex, Inglaterra filho de John du Plessis 7th Earl of Warwick e Christian Sanford. Ele casou-se com Isabel Biset em outubro de 1249, em Stottesdon, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 7 de agosto de 1292, em Stottesden, Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 55 anos, e foi sepultado em St Peter's Church, Hook Norton, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.1237–7 August 1292: с 8 января 1237 по 7 января 1238–7 августа 1292 г.
ru.Saint Peters Church, Hook Norton, Cherwe: ст питир'с чурч, хук нортон, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.St Peters Church, Hook Norton, Oxfordshi: ст питир'с чурч, хук нортон, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Hugh du Plessis Baron of Arundel родился в 1237 г. в местности арундель кастл, суссекс, Англия. Его родителей звали John du Plessis 7th Earl of Warwick и Christian Sanford. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel Biset октябрь 1249 г. в местности стоттесдон, шропшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер 7 августа 1292 г. в местности стоттесден, шропшир, Англия в возрасте 55 лет и был похоронен в местности ст питир'с чурч, хук нортон, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Hugh du Plessis Baron of Arundel nació en 1237, en Arundel Castle, Sussex, Inglaterra como hijo de John du Plessis 7th Earl of Warwick y Christian Sanford. Se casó con Isabel Biset en octubre de 1249, en Stottesdon, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió el 7 de agosto de 1292, en Stottesden, Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultado en St Peter's Church, Hook Norton, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
